div1.entryFree: Porpentine (most M. Edd. porcupine), the animal Hystrix cristata, now called porcupine: H6B III, 1, 363. Troil. II, 1, 27 (applied to Thersites as a term of reproach, probably on account of the prevailing opinion that the porcupine could dart its quills). Hml. I, 5, 20. Name of an inn: Err. III, 1, 116. III, 2, 172. IV, 1, 49. V, 222. V, 222
